title: pastinaca aspera
intro: Pastinaca aspera, also known as rough hog's fennel or wild parsnip, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its fern-like foliage and yellowish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pastinaca aspera include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and can also be used fresh in salads or as a culinary herb.
